title: carduus nutans subsp. sporadum
intro: Carduus nutans subsp. sporadum, also known as wild artichoke or cynara, is a type of thistle plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits. The leaves and flowers of Carduus nutans subsp. sporadum have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including high cholesterol, arthritis, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to support liver and digestive health.
